assertions.expectation: Recommends upgrading the L4 inference pool and A100 pool separately with different strategies
assertions.reason: The response clearly recommends upgrading pools separately - L4 pool first (less critical) with 25% initial node percentage, then A100 pool with 20% initial node percentage and longer timeout (10800s vs 7200s)

assertions.expectation: For L4 inference pool: recommends surge upgrade with maxSurge=1 or 2 to maintain capacity during rolling replacement
assertions.reason: The response recommends autoscaled blue-green strategy, not surge upgrade with maxSurge parameters. It uses blue-green-initial-node-percentage instead of maxSurge settings

assertions.expectation: Addresses GPU driver version compatibility — auto-installed drivers must be compatible with inference framework (TensorRT, vLLM, etc.)
assertions.reason: The response explicitly addresses GPU driver compatibility in Phase 1, mentioning that GKE 1.32 may update CUDA drivers and recommends testing inference frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch) with 1.32's CUDA version in staging

assertions.expectation: For A100 fine-tuning pool: recommends scheduling the upgrade during a gap between fine-tuning jobs
assertions.reason: The response specifically recommends upgrading A100 pool 'during low fine-tuning activity' and provides Option A for 'Natural Completion' where jobs finish naturally during the blue-green upgrade

assertions.expectation: Notes that GPU VMs do not support live migration — inference pods will restart during node upgrade, causing brief latency spikes
assertions.reason: The response does not mention that GPU VMs don't support live migration or that inference pods will restart causing latency spikes. It focuses on blue-green strategy maintaining capacity but doesn't address pod restart implications

assertions.expectation: Recommends PDBs on inference deployments to ensure enough replicas stay available during the rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: The response includes verification of PDBs in the pre-upgrade phase with the command 'kubectl get pdb -A -o wide' and mentions PDBs as part of workload assessment

assertions.expectation: Addresses auto-scaler interaction — new nodes created by auto-scaler during upgrade will be at the old version until their pool upgrade completes
assertions.reason: The response mentions auto-scaling throughout but does not specifically address that new nodes created by auto-scaler during upgrade will be at the old version until pool upgrade completes

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing driver + CUDA compatibility in staging before production upgrade
assertions.reason: The response explicitly recommends testing in staging: 'Test in staging: Verify your inference frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, etc.) work with 1.32's CUDA version' and lists this as a risk mitigation strategy
